entries.definition:
كتاب الطاء

طوط

(طير) الطاء والياء والراء أصلٌ واحدٌ يدلُّ على خِفّة الشَّيءِ في الهواءِ.

ṭūbā to a borrowing from Hindi. Of this root, six forms occur 50 times the Qurʾan: طَابَ ṭāba three times; طُوبَى ṭūbā once; طَيِّب ṭayyib 13 times; طَيِّبُون ṭayyibūn three times; طَيِّبَة ṭayyibatun nine times and طَيِّبَات ṭayyibāt 21 times.
طَابَ ṭāba i [v. intrans.] 1 to be or become desirable or agreeable (4:3) فَانكِحُوا مَا طَابَ لَكُم مِّنَ النِّسَاءِ then marry of [the other] women those who seem agreeable to you 2 to become good, to be good, to become pious, to be pious (39:73) وَقَالَ لَهُمْ خَزَنَتُهَا سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ طِبْتُمْ فَادْخُلُوهَا خَالِدِينَ and its keepers say to them, ‘Peace be upon you, you have been good, enter it, [to dwell] for ever’; *(4:4) فَإِن طِبْنَ لَكُمْ عَن شَيْءٍ مِّنْهُ نَفْسًا but if they are, of their own accord, happy to give up some of it for you.

طُوبَى ṭūbā [v. n./n.] variously interpreted as: the name of a tree in Paradise, borrowing from Ethiopian or Hindi meaning paradise, borrowing from Persian meaning golden, or noun meaning happiness, joy, goodness or affluence (13:29) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ طُوبَى لَهُمْ وَحُسْنُ مَآبٍ those who believe and do righteous deeds, joy is theirs and for them an excellent homecoming.

طَيِّب ṭayyib [quasi-act. part.; pl. طَيِّبُون ṭayyibūn; fem. طَيِّبَة ṭayyibatun; fem. pl. طَيِّبَات ṭayyibāt] 1 good (14:24) كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً a good word is like a good tree 2 the Pious, the chaste (24:26) الطَّيِّبَاتُ لِلطَّيِّبِينَ وَالطَّيِّبُونَ لِلطَّيِّبَاتِ the chaste women are for the chaste men, and the chaste men are for the chaste women 3 clean (4:43) فَتَيَمَّمُوا صَعِيدًا طَيِّبًا then select clean earth 4 wholesome (16:114) فَكُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ حَلَالًا طَيِّبًا so eat of the wholesome and lawful things God has provided for you 5 favourable, fair (10:22) حَتَّى إِذَا كُنتُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَجَرَيْنَ بِهِم بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ till when you are in the ships and they sail with them [you] on a fair wind 6 agreeable, pleasant (61:12) وَمَسَاكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ into pleasant dwellings in the Gardens of Eternity.

entries.definition:
ṭ-y-r flying thing/creature, a bird, an insect, to fly, to fly away; to hasten; to rejoice; to scatter, to spread out; bad omen, augury; deed. Of this root, six forms occur 29 times in the Qurʾan: يَطِيرُ yaṭīr once; تَطَيَّرَ taṭayyara once; اطَّيَّرَ ʾiṭṭayyara twice; طَيْر tayr
582
